obligation_text: Providers shall establish and document a post-market monitoring system in a manner that is proportionate to the nature of the AI technologies and the risks of the high-risk AI system.
scoring_text: Skill in designing AI post-market monitoring systems, including proportionality assessment for AI technologies, monitoring system architecture, and documentation of monitoring approaches appropriate to risk levels.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing AI post-market monitoring systems, including proportionality assessment for AI technologies, monitoring system architecture, and documentation of monitoring approaches appropriate to risk levels.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 72(1) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.729, cosine: 0.667). Secondary: RM-S-001 (RM, cosine: 0.655), DG-T-007 (DG, cosine: 0.654).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.3/10 (raw 0.729). Shared vocabulary: [ai, documentation, risk, skill]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [approaches, appropriate, architecture, assessment, designing, levels] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, evaluation, harm] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.903 (P=0.894, R=0.912). Balanced P/R — the 25-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (90.6%). Semantic tension between "Skill in designing AI post-market monitoring systems, including proportionality" and "Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identifi". Despite STS-based 'Equal' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.667 < STS 0.729 (gap -0.062). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning between [approaches, appropriate, architecture] and [assessments, bias, conducting] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 20.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, documentation, risk, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [approaches, appropriate, architecture, assessment, designing, levels]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, evaluation, harm].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 90.6% on Equal classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.729 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
